title: Pouvoirs de signature de la Nouvelle-Zélande

pages.text:
.

WHEREAS New Zealand is a party to the Convention relating to International Exhibitions, 1928, as amended by a Protocol signed at Paris on 10 May 1948, (hereinafter referred to as "the Convertion")

AND WHEREAS a Protocol modifying Article IV of the

Convention has been drawn up for signature on behalf of the Governments parties to the Convention

AND WHEREAS the said Protocol is to be open for signature in Paris from 16 November 1966 until 17 December 1966

AND WHEREAS it is expedient that a fit person should be invested with full powers to sign the Protocol on behalf of the Government of New Zealand

I, KEITH JACKA HOLYOAKE, Minister of External Affairs of New Zealand, HEREBY CERTIFY that RICHARD LEWIS HUTCHENS,

Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary of New Zealand to the Republic of France, is by these presents NAMED,

CONSTITUTED AND APPOINTED to sign the Protocol modifying

Article IV of the Convention relating to International

Exhibitions, 1928, on behalf of the Government of

New Zealand

• AND I HEREBY FURTHER CERTIFY that the Protocol, when signed by the said RICHARD LEWIS HUTCHENS WIll, subject to ratification by the Government of New Zealand, be acknowledged by the Government of New Zealand in the fullest manner.

/IN

2.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF I have signed these presents and affixed hereto the seal of the Minister of External

Affairs of New Zealand.

SIGNED AND SEALED at Wellington this day of October

1966

18th

Minister of External Affairs of New Zealand
